page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC 9T24 processors running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a physical core, eliminating hyperthreading resource contention.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers between instances and cloud services.

Given its high core count and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.c8a.32xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance. This configuration is suitable for high-performance computing, batch processing, scientific modeling, video encoding, and large-scale build systems.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8a.32xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance designed for high-performance workloads. Powered by KVM virtualization, it features 128 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T24 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance provides 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps. This resource allocation makes the instance highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as batch processing, scientific simulations, and large-scale compilation where dedicated CPU execution is critical and memory requirements are moderate.
